briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: To provide a universal fallback metadata extractor that works for ANY file format, returning OS-level file attributes (name, size, timestamps, MIME type) and optional base64 encoding.

detaileddescription.para: \par \b Scope\/ \: Extracting basic file metadata for all file formats, base64-encoding of file content. \par \b Input\/ \: file_path via extract(), optional parameters dict. \par \b Output\/ \: dict with keys: file_name, temporary_file_name, file_type, size, access_time, created_time, modified_time, [base64_encode]. \par \b Links\/ \: [USES_API(7): AbstractMetadataExtractor, os.stat, dedoc.utils.get_file_mime_type] \par \b Invariants\/ \:

listitem.para: _get_base_meta_information ALWAYS calls os.stat and returns a dict with 7 fixed keys. \par \b Rationale\/ \:

detaileddescription.para: Q: Why have a universal extractor that matches everything? A: Serves as the safety net in the extraction chain. Even if no format-specific extractor handles a file, BaseMetadataExtractor provides essential OS metadata. \par \b Changes\/ \:
LAST_CHANGE: [v1.0.0 — Semantic template markup and LDD logging added.] \par \b Module Map\/ \:
CLASS 8[Universal metadata extractor for any file format] => BaseMetadataExtractor \par \b Use Cases\/ \:
